description_long: This object is a stone grave marker featuring a prominent Celtic cross mounted atop a tapered rectangular pedestal. The cross is circular at its center, with the arms extending through the circle, and the entire structure sits on a stepped, square base. The surface of the stone is weathered, with visible scratches and discoloration, giving it an aged appearance. The pedestal is solid and robust, with no visible openings or compartments, and the cross is seamlessly integrated into the top of the pedestal. The base is slightly wider than the pedestal, providing stability and a sense of permanence. There are no visible inscriptions or engravings on the stone, and the overall color is a mottled gray with subtle white highlights.
description: A weathered stone grave marker with a Celtic cross atop a tapered pedestal and a stepped square base, all in mottled gray stone.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the Celtic cross and the broad, sloped front of the pedestal.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the cross and pedestal, emphasizing the depth of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cross and pedestal, similar in shape to the front but without any visible markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the stepped base.
